/allocation.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="alloc.title">Allocation, cubing & batching — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="The openWCS outbound allocation engine: pick-location allocation with UoM breakdown against location-scoped ATP, multi-size cubing into shippers (largest-first, line-traceable), per-shipper dispatch labels, and batch picking. Built today.">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#061812">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/allocation.html">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">

  <meta property="og:description" content="Outbound allocation with UoM breakdown, multi-size cubing into shippers, per-shipper dispatch labels, and batch picking.">
  <meta property="og:type" content="article">
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">

/architecture.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="arch.title">How openWCS is built — Architecture — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="A plain-language tour of how openWCS is built: a set of small specialised services that each own one job, talking over a shared event log, sitting between your WMS/ERP and the warehouse floor. No jargon — just how the pieces fit together.">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#061812">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/architecture.html">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">

  <meta property="og:description" content="A plain-language tour of how openWCS is built: small specialised services that each own one job, talking over a shared event log, between your WMS/ERP and the warehouse floor.">

  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">

/asrs.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="asrs.title">ASRS — automated storage & retrieval — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="How openWCS drives automated storage & retrieval (shuttle/crane ASRS, AutoStore, AMR goods-to-person): aisle/side/cell addressing, store & retrieve cycles, in-aisle depth behaviour, retrieval to goods-to-person stations, and empty-HU management. The slotting logic is a separate WCS function.">

  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">

  <meta property="og:description" content="Driving shuttle/crane ASRS, AutoStore and AMR goods-to-person: aisle/side/cell addressing, store & retrieve cycles, in-aisle behaviour, GTP retrieval, empty-HU management.">

  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">

/automation.html.headMeta:

  <meta name="description" content="The openWCS automation hub: vendor-neutral control of conveyors, ASRS storage and goods-to-person stations over a uniform device contract — with AMR and AutoStore adapters on the roadmap. Each family tagged Built or Roadmap.">

/conveyors.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="conv.title">Conveyor routing — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="How openWCS routes handling units across conveyors: a vendor-neutral topology graph, WCS pathfinding to sequenced targets, per-loop capacity limits, a 2D top-down + 3D layout editor where routing is inferred from physical placement, and topology learning from live scan traffic.">

  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">

/gtp.html.headMeta:

/.headMeta:
<title>openWCS — the open-source Warehouse Control System you actually control</title>
  <meta name="description" content="openWCS is an open-source Warehouse Control System: vendor-neutral equipment control, visual conveyor routing, and admin-designed BPMN processes. The transparent alternative to locked-in WCS apps from integrators.">
  <meta name="keywords" content="WCS, warehouse control system, open source WCS, AGPL, conveyor routing, ASRS, AMR, AutoStore, BPMN, material handling, warehouse automation, vendor-neutral">
  <meta name="author" content="openWCS">
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#061812">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/">
  <!-- Icons -->
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="favicon.png">
  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="openWCS">
  <meta property="og:title" content="openWCS — the open Warehouse Control System">
  <meta property="og:description" content="Orchestrate conveyors, ASRS, AMRs and AutoStore between your WMS/ERP and the machines — open, vendor-neutral, and yours to change.">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/">
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="800">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="447">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="openWCS — open-source warehouse control system orchestrating an automated warehouse">

  <meta property="og:locale:alternate" content="de_DE">
  <meta property="og:locale:alternate" content="fr_FR">
  <meta property="og:locale:alternate" content="es_ES">
  <!-- Twitter / X -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="openWCS — the open Warehouse Control System">
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="The transparent, vendor-neutral alternative to locked-in WCS apps from integrators. Open source (AGPL-3.0), self-hosted, yours to change.">
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">

  }
  </script>

/roadmap.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="rm.title">Roadmap — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="The openWCS roadmap: what is shipped, in progress, next up and being explored — a living timeline rendered straight from roadmap.md, kept honest against the codebase.">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#061812">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/roadmap.html">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">
  <meta property="og:title" content="Roadmap — openWCS">
  <meta property="og:description" content="Shipped, in progress, next up and exploring — the openWCS roadmap as a living timeline, generated from roadmap.md and kept honest against what's actually built.">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/roadmap.html">
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/openwcs.png">
/roadmap.html.scripts:
<script>
  /* Render the roadmap timeline from public/roadmap.md — the single source of truth.
     Keep the file in roadmap.md format; this just parses and draws it. No build step. */
  (function () {
    var STATUS = {
      done:      { label: 'Shipped',     card: 'rm-done',      pill: 'pill-built' },
      active:    { label: 'In progress', card: 'rm-active',    pill: 'pill-active' },
      planned:   { label: 'Planned',     card: 'rm-planned',   pill: 'pill-planned' },
      exploring: { label: 'Exploring',   card: 'rm-exploring', pill: 'pill-exploring' }
    };

    function parse(md) {
      md = md.replace(/<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g, '');          // drop HTML comments (the editing guide)
      var phases = [], cur = null;
      md.split(/\r?\n/).forEach(function (raw) {
        var t = raw.trim();
        if (!t) return;
        var h = t.match(/^##\s+(.+)$/);
        if (h) { cur = { title: h[1].trim(), sub: '', items: [] }; phases.push(cur); return; }
        if (!cur) return;
        var s = t.match(/^>\s+(.+)$/);
        if (s) { cur.sub = s[1].trim(); return; }
        var it = t.match(/^-\s*\[(done|active|planned|exploring)\]\s*([\s\S]+)$/i);
        if (it) {
          var bits = it[2].split('::');
          cur.items.push({
            status: it[1].toLowerCase(),
            title: bits[0].trim(),
            desc: bits.slice(1).join('::').trim()
          });
        }
      });
      return phases;
    }

    function el(tag, cls, text) {
      var n = document.createElement(tag);
      if (cls) n.className = cls;
      if (text != null) n.textContent = text;          // textContent → safe against any markup in the .md
      return n;
    }

    function render(phases) {
      var root = document.getElementById('roadmap');
      root.innerHTML = '';
      var side = 0;                                     // running counter → alternating left/right
      phases.forEach(function (ph) {
        // Phase milestone — a labelled station on the spine.
        var phase = el('div', 'tl-phase');
        phase.setAttribute('data-reveal', '');
        var mile = el('div', 'tl-milestone');
        mile.appendChild(el('span', 'tl-mdot'));
        var ml = el('div', 'tl-mlabel');
        ml.appendChild(el('h2', null, ph.title));
        if (ph.sub) ml.appendChild(el('p', 'tl-msub', ph.sub));
        mile.appendChild(ml);
        phase.appendChild(mile);
        root.appendChild(phase);

        // Items — each a card pinned to the spine by a node, alternating sides.
        ph.items.forEach(function (item) {
          var meta = STATUS[item.status] || STATUS.planned;
          var row = el('div', 'tl-item ' + meta.card + ' ' + (side++ % 2 ? 'tl-right' : 'tl-left'));
          row.setAttribute('data-reveal', '');
          row.appendChild(el('span', 'tl-conn'));
          row.appendChild(el('span', 'tl-node'));

          var card = el('article', 'rm-card ' + meta.card);
          var ch = el('div', 'rm-card-head');
          ch.appendChild(el('h3', null, item.title));
          ch.appendChild(el('span', 'pill ' + meta.pill, meta.label));
          card.appendChild(ch);
          if (item.desc) card.appendChild(el('p', null, item.desc));
          row.appendChild(card);
          root.appendChild(row);
        });
      });
      revealNew(root);
    }

    /* i18n.js already ran its reveal() over the static page before this fetch resolved,
       so wire up the freshly-injected phases ourselves. */
    function revealNew(root) {
      var els = root.querySelectorAll('[data-reveal]');
      var reduce = window.matchMedia && matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
      if (!('IntersectionObserver' in window) || reduce) {
        els.forEach(function (e) { e.classList.add('in'); });
        return;
      }
      var io = new IntersectionObserver(function (entries) {
        entries.forEach(function (en) {
          if (en.isIntersecting) { en.target.classList.add('in'); io.unobserve(en.target); }
        });
      }, { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: '0px 0px -8% 0px' });
      els.forEach(function (e) { io.observe(e); });
    }

    function fail() {
      var root = document.getElementById('roadmap');
      root.innerHTML = '';
      var p = el('p', null);
      p.style.color = 'var(--text-dim)';
      p.appendChild(document.createTextNode("Couldn't load the roadmap here. Read it directly: "));
      var a = el('a', null, 'roadmap.md');
      a.href = 'roadmap.md';
      p.appendChild(a);
      p.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' · '));
      var b = el('a', null, 'on GitHub');
      b.href = 'https://github.com/brettljausn-ai/openwcs/blob/main/public/roadmap.md';
      p.appendChild(b);
      p.appendChild(document.createTextNode('.'));
      root.appendChild(p);
    }

    fetch('roadmap.md', { cache: 'no-cache' })
      .then(function (r) { if (!r.ok) throw new Error(r.status); return r.text(); })
      .then(function (md) {
        var phases = parse(md);
        if (!phases.length) throw new Error('empty');
        render(phases);
      })
      .catch(fail);
  })();
  </script>

/security.html.headMeta:
<title data-i18n="sec.title">Security & access control — openWCS</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Security in openWCS: gateway JWT validation against a Keycloak realm, per-endpoint role-based access control across every service from a shared role→permission catalog, inter-service identity propagation — all toggleable so you start simple and lock down for production.">
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#061812">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://brettljausn-ai.github.io/openwcs/security.html">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png">
  <meta property="og:title" content="Security & access control — openWCS">
